heading: The Tainter Curve
info: Solving problems eventually costs more than the reward.
text: Joseph Tainter discovered that civilizations reach a mathematical threshold where solving problems costs more than the problems are worth.

heading: Roman Cashflow
info: The army size grew faster than conquest returns.
text: Roman historical data reveals a bending curve: they expanded to fund complexity, but the army size grew faster than the plunder could sustain.

heading: Diminishing Returns
info: Every new bureaucratic layer yields smaller benefits.
text: This is the law of diminishing returns. After a certain point, every new layer of bureaucracy actually yields a smaller increment of return.

heading: Thermodynamic Death
info: Complexity is a ratchet that only turns one way.
text: Our modern social order is growing increasingly top-heavy. Bureaucracy doesn't just fail; it becomes a thermodynamic inevitability.
